notes: Canadian Armed Forces shallow-water diver candidates (n=241); incidence density 8.68/1000 person-days; 56 individuals with 60 OBT events; person-days back-calculated from stated rate; military confined-training cohort is analog-equivalent

Italian Air Force pilots (n=335); single-day hypobaric chamber training event; 8 barotitis media events (2.4%); pre-screened healthy subjects only; good analog for pressurized confined-environment exposure
Italian aircrew (n=314); single-day hypobaric chamber training up to 35000 ft; 7 acute barotitis events (2.3%); 28 delayed ear-pain events also recorded; row uses acute confirmed only

USA terrestrial altitude research chamber; 33-yr cohort; 23656 exposures; ear block rate 0.93/100 exposures; EST events = 220; lower bound analog estimate - research volunteers prescreened; best long-run denominator available
Israeli Air Force altitude chamber; 1627 trainees 2015-2019; overall adverse-event rate 5.59%; 69.3% were ear/sinus barotrauma; EST 63 ear/sinus events from 91 total AEs

Japan Air Self-Defense Force hypobaric chamber; 39-yr cohort; 29677 trainees / 58454 exposures; ear pain listed as most prevalent incident; 6.3% overall incident rate; EST ear events using conservative 4% ear-specific fraction
US military multiplace HBOT; 111 patients / 2394 sessions; 35 symptomatic barotrauma cases (3.05/100); 95% ear-affected; EST 33 ear events; HBOT confined-chamber environment is analog-adjacent
India tertiary hospital HBOT; 100 patients / 1216 sessions; 19 patients (19%) developed otitic barotrauma; person-days estimated as session count; patient-level event count used

Taiwan Navy; 3608 pressure-test trials (hyperbaric chamber to 112 fsw); 435 failures (12.06%); 365 ear barotrauma events (96.56% of 378 with recorded cause); high-pressure military selection - upper-bound estimate for analog confined pressure exposure
Eye Debris events from NASA LSAH (Shuttle+ISS Exp1-13); represents superficial FB not true intraocular penetration (ICD S05.5); zero true IOFB events documented; 70 corneal abrasions also logged

WV workers comp FY1998; penetrating eyeball ICD rate 3.7/100k employees/yr; 652956 workers; ~300x higher lambda vs analog population due to unshielded industrial metalwork; analog population estimate offset factor ~0.03-0.10

NETS Registry 1985-1991; 635 occupational penetrating injuries/7yr at 48 centers; 35% IOFB; back-calculated from national rate ~2/100k/yr; US working pop ~120M; ~50-100x higher than analog due to unprotected industrial work

GBD 2021 global ASIR 354.39/100k/yr (all IOFB including superficial); ~20% estimated true penetrating globe (ICD S05.5); high-SDI ASIR 509.26/100k/yr; global general population denominator; analog crew expected ~50-100x lower rate due to PPE screening and low-risk activities

GBD 2019 global IOFB; 46.63M total IOFB cases; ~20% estimated penetrating globe; ASIR declining trend -0.93%/yr EAPC; high-income North America has higher ASIR; general pop rate ~500-1000x higher than analog mission candidate

SW China (Sichuan pop ~83M) hospitalized IOFB 2009-2018; annual incidence 0.14/100k; all cases are true intraocular penetrating (surgical removal); predominantly metalworking; analog population expected ~10-50x lower due to PPE and selection
NASA LSAH cohort 242 astronauts 5-yr post-flight; 4 of 22 shoulder injuries classified as rotator cuff sprain/strain; lambda=0.0000091/pd; directly applicable analog population
In-flight MSK injuries NASA space program through 2006; shoulder fraction 15% and sprain/strain 18% of shoulder both estimated; high uncertainty; use as lower-bound check only

NASA astronaut cohort n=338 vs 347540 working professionals 10-yr follow-up; shoulder consultation and surgery endpoint (not sprain-specific); sprain/strain event count estimated from 18% sub-fraction; use for plausibility check only

Healthcare workers n=4406 19898 PY IR=17.9/1000PY all shoulder disorders; sprain/strain fraction 18% estimated; physically active occupational cohort is moderate analog proxy; selection-bias vs analog ~0.5x (healthier-worker effect partially offset by physical demand)

Systematic review 8 incidence studies general-pop shoulder pain median IR 37.8/1000 PY (range 7.7-62); sprain/strain subset 18% estimated; broad case definition includes tendinopathy and pain syndromes not just sprain; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.3x vs analog astronaut cohort

NEISS 2019 ED-based shoulder injuries (S40-S49) USA; sprain/strain fraction 30% estimated; excludes S43 dislocation; severe under-reporting vs true incidence (ED-only capture); general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.2x vs analog cohort

Nationwide Japanese judo insurance DB 2010-2019 adolescents 12-18y; elbow sprain rate 5.4-6.8/1000 athlete-years (mean 6.1); sprains=67.6% of 4614 elbow injuries; high-physical-demand sport proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

US HS baseball High-School-RIO surveillance 2005-2015 1734198 AEs; 150 total elbow injuries; ligament sprains estimated 30% of elbow injuries; AE as athletic-exposure day unit; sport-specific overhead-athlete proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

US HS baseball+softball HS-RIO surveillance 2005-2015 4059418 AEs; 289 elbow injuries; ~40% sprains estimated from overhead-sport literature; AE as athletic-exposure day unit; sport-specific proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

US HS baseball+softball HS-RIO surveillance 2005-2019 14 seasons 5738470 AEs; 518 total elbow/forearm injuries; ~40% sprains estimated; largest multi-season HS overhead-athlete dataset; sport-specific proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

NCAA-ISP 5-season surveillance 2009-2014 11 sports 2119318 AEs; UCL/ligament sprains=26% of 373 elbow injuries; mixed college sports (wrestling highest at 6.0/10k AEs); AE as athletic-exposure day unit; collegiate athlete proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort
NCAA athletes 25 sports 2009-2015; hip flexor+adductor strains 1.60+1.29/10000 AE; sports cohort proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort
NCAA athletes 25 sports 2009-2014; 53.06/100k AE overall hip injury rate; strain subset 50% estimated; sports cohort proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort
UEFA professional football 47 clubs 15 seasons; 1.0/1000h hip+groin time-loss; all soft-tissue strains/sprains; sports cohort proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

BJSM meta-analysis 71 studies 9.4M h exposure 5914 injuries; 0.71/1000h HGI incidence; 85% football; adductor-related 0.41/1000h; sports cohort proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

PAC-12 Division I 5 years 16276 person-years 15609 athletes 2768 hip/groin injuries; direct person-year denominator; sports cohort proxy; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

NEISS 2009-2018 US ED data; wrist injuries 182/100k/yr; 12.3% strains/sprains; wrist sprain rate EST 20.4/100k/yr; ER-presenting only underestimates true community burden; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort
Meta-analysis 206 studies; ankle sprains pooled 429.4/100k/yr (reference ceiling); wrist sprain context bounding; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

Danish population-based ED study 1yr; overall wrist trauma 69/10000/yr; non-fracture soft-tissue EST 42/10000/yr; sprain fraction EST 20% = 8.4/10000/yr = 84/100k/yr; catchment pop ~100000; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

Dutch GP primary-care cohort 200000 patients 5yr; wrist ligament injury 0.1/1000 person-years (95% CI 0.04-0.12); primary care only floor estimate; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

US Military Academy cadets 2005-2008; 90 ICD-coded wrist sprains in 4400-person cohort over 4yr; rate 511/100k/yr reflects high-activity physically-selected population; retained as upper-bound reference; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort

Dutch Injury Surveillance System 2016; 160250 hand+wrist ED visits; incidence 11/1000 males 8/1000 females; wrist sprain EST 13% of wrist visits; general-pop proxy; selection-bias ~0.5x expected vs analog cohort
